title: AgiBot kicks off Hong Kong IPO as top humanoid maker by shipments
summary: Shanghai humanoid maker AgiBot has formally begun its Hong Kong listing process, backed by world-leading 2025 shipments and a valuation target near US$6 billion.
body:
AgiBot (Zhiyuan Robotics), the Shanghai-based humanoid robot maker co-founded by former Huawei "genius youth" Peng Zhihui, has formally started its Hong Kong listing process, according to a report by IT Home on Thursday. The company shipped more humanoid robots than any competitor worldwide in 2025 — 5,168 units by analyst firm Omdia's count — and rolled its 10,000th robot off the line in March 2026.

## From denials to filing
The move ends a year of will-they-won't-they. AgiBot denied Hong Kong IPO reports twice in 2025, even stating it had no backdoor-listing plans within 36 months after taking control of A-share company Swancor Advanced Materials for 2.1 billion yuan. Earlier Reuters reporting said the company had mandated CICC, CITIC Securities, and Morgan Stanley, targeting a valuation of HK$40–50 billion (US$5.1–6.4 billion), a 15–25 percent float that could raise over US$1 billion, and a listing as soon as the third quarter of 2026 — a timeline the newly confirmed process start now makes plausible.

## The embodied-AI capital wave
Founded in February 2023 by Peng and former Huawei vice president Deng Taihua, AgiBot counts Tencent, JD.com, SAIC, LG Electronics, and Mirae Asset among its backers, and gained political visibility when President Xi Jinping inspected its robots in Shanghai. A successful listing would make it only the second humanoid-focused company on the Hong Kong exchange after UBTech, and the first of the new generation of embodied-AI unicorns — a cohort that includes Unitree, which is pursuing its own listing — to reach public markets.

## Why it matters
Humanoid robotics has absorbed enormous private capital on thin revenue, and AgiBot's IPO will be the first real price discovery for the sector's frontrunner: a company claiming both global shipment leadership and roughly 4 billion yuan in expected 2026 revenue. How public investors value that story will calibrate funding conditions for every embodied-AI startup in China — and signal whether the humanoid boom can graduate from government showcases to a durable business.
title_zh: 2025 年出货全球第一,智元机器人启动港股上市流程
summary_zh: IT之家报道智元机器人正式启动赴港上市流程:2025 年人形机器人出货 5168 台全球居首,估值瞄准 400 至 500 亿港元。
body_zh:
据 IT之家周四报道,由前华为'天才少年'彭志辉(稚晖君)联合创办的上海人形机器人公司智元机器人,已正式启动赴港上市流程。这家公司 2025 年人形机器人出货量全球第一——按分析机构 Omdia 统计为 5168 台——并在 2026 年 3 月迎来第 1 万台机器人下线。

## 从两度否认到正式启动
这一步为持续一年的'上市罗生门'画上句号。智元在 2025 年曾两次否认赴港 IPO 传闻,甚至在斥资 21 亿元控股 A 股公司上纬新材后声明'36 个月内不存在借壳上市安排'。据路透社此前报道,公司已聘请中金公司、中信证券与摩根士丹利,目标估值 400 至 500 亿港元(约 51 至 64 亿美元),拟发行 15% 至 25% 股份、募资或超 10 亿美元,最快 2026 年三季度挂牌——如今流程正式启动,这一时间表变得可信。

## 具身智能的资本大潮
智元 2023 年 2 月由彭志辉与前华为副总裁邓泰华创立,股东阵容包括腾讯、京东、上汽、LG 电子和韩国未来资产集团,此前更因领导人视察其上海产线而声名大噪。若成功上市,它将成为继优必选之后港交所第二家人形机器人公司,也是新一代具身智能独角兽(同赛道的宇树也在推进上市)中第一个走向公开市场的玩家。

## 为什么重要
人形机器人赛道吸走了海量一级市场资金,营收却普遍单薄。智元 IPO 将是这个行业领跑者的第一次真实定价:一家同时手握全球出货第一和 2026 年约 40 亿元预期营收的公司,公开市场投资者愿意给多少估值,将直接校准中国所有具身智能创业公司的融资环境——也将回答人形机器人热潮能否从政府展台走向可持续生意。

sources.title: 智元启动赴港上市流程,2025 年通用人形机器人出货量全球第一 — IT之家
Exclusive: Chinese robot maker AgiBot plans Hong Kong IPO — Reuters via Yahoo Finance
